title: AMC Theatres Sees Revenue Rise As Hollywood Box Office Bounces Back
full_text: × Skip to main content By Etan Vlessing Plus Icon Etan Vlessing Canada Bureau Chief View All July 20, 2026 4:26am AMC Empire 25 in Times Square, New York. The Hollywood Reporter An ongoing and apparently sustained Hollywood box office recovery underpinned solid revenue growth for AMC Theatres during the second quarter. Overall revenue was up 14 percent to $1.59 billion, compared to $1.39 billion in the same period of 2025, even as AMC increased its net loss to $11.4 million, against a year-earlier $4.7 million. The adjusted EBITDA came to $320.6 million, compared to $189.2 million. The second quarter results at AMC follow the exhibition industry seeing gains from steady supply of Hollywood tentpole releases like Toy Story 5 and The Super Mario Galaxy Movie , and surprise horror pics like Backrooms and Obsession , to get film lovers back in the habit of going to the multiplex in pre-pandemic numbers. Just this weekend, Christopher Nolan’s epic drama The Odyssey revived the summer box office with this year’s biggest live-action debut and the Oscar-winning filmmaker’s highest-grossing global launch of $124 million. That’s allowed exhibitors to look to full-year 2026 box office likely to exceed the comparable performance for last year, and possibly set a new annual record. “Both our U.S. and European businesses showed great progress in the second quarter. The momentum in the total industry-wide domestic box office was undeniable, reaching approximately $2.99 billion, up 10.7 percent from last year’s second quarter, making this the biggest box office quarter in seven years and the fifth biggest quarter ever,” AMC Theatres CEO Adam Aron said in a statement on Monday morning. U.S. market attendance rose 12 percent to 52.2 million patrons, compared to 46.8 million in 2025, while international markets attendance was also up by 18 percent to 18.7 million cinemagoers, against 16 million patrons. Aron also talked up fortunes for his debt-laden company as it looks to recover from the impact of pandemic-era box office disruptions: “This demonstrates the inherent operating leverage in our business model at a time of rising revenues, combined with the power of AMC’s market leading position, the appeal of our theatres, the increasing numbers of our premium offerings, the prowess of our marketing programs and our ability to keep a tight lid on our costs. The theater chain’s chief added, “Finally, after some admittedly tough years as our industry recovered only slowly from the ravages of COVID-19 and its aftermath, the relentless focus of AMC on delighting our guests as we execute with all cylinders blazing is reflected in our record-setting second quarter financial results.” THR

title: Andy Burnham Becomes U.K. Prime Minister, Succeeding Keir Starmer
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 4:24am PT Andy Burnham Becomes U.K. Prime Minister, Succeeding Keir Starmer By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest Andy Burnham Becomes U.K. Prime Minister, Succeeding Keir Starmer 18 minutes ago ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 5 hours ago See All Getty Images Andy Burnham became Prime Minister of the United Kingdom on Monday, taking over from Keir Starmer in a handover choreographed around a visit to Buckingham Palace, where Starmer formally resigned and King Charles III then invited Burnham to form a government. Before heading to the Palace, Starmer delivered a farewell address outside 10 Downing Street, flanked by colleagues and staff who greeted him with cheers and applause. He framed his six and a half years leading the Labour Party and two as Prime Minister around the 2024 landslide election win, pointing to falling immigration numbers, shorter National Health Service waiting times and children lifted out of poverty as markers of his record. He said the job’s most humbling aspect was witnessing everyday “grit, decency, and compassion” across the country, and closed by wishing Burnham “every success,” pledging him “full support” before thanking his family, staff and the wider Labour Party. Related Stories Does California Attorney General Want to Force Paramount to Sell Off CNN? Rob Bonta Claims 'I Have No Idea' Where That Speculation 'Comes From' Paramount Blasts States' Lawsuit Aimed at Blocking Warner Bros. Deal, Claims Litigation Will 'Shield' Netflix and Tech Companies From 'Much-Needed Competition' “My work is done,” Starmer said. “It has been the privilege of my life to serve you and this great country, as Prime Minister, and I am confident that Britain is now stronger and fairer than it was two years ago.” Popular on Variety Starmer’s final days in office brought a run of international recognition. On July 14, French President Emmanuel Macron awarded him the Legion of Honor for his work with France on European security. Two days later, on a final visit to Kyiv, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy presented him with the Order of Freedom, Ukraine’s highest honor for a foreign national, citing his support for the country throughout the war with Russia. “I go with good grace, I go with a smile, and I go proud of everything that we have achieved,” Starmer said. Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, had been confirmed as Labour leader unopposed on July 17, after securing nominations from 379 of the party’s 403 MPs. His confirmation capped a five-week run from his return to Parliament via a by-election win in Makerfield to his installation as the country’s seventh prime minister in a decade. Burnham was the only declared candidate in the leadership contest, which followed a rebellion among Labour lawmakers against Starmer. After Starmer formally resigned as Prime Minister at Buckingham Palace, Burnham followed him and met King Charles III. He has formally become the U.K.’s 59th Prime Minister. A statement from Buckingham Palace said: “His Majesty received in audience the Rt Hon Andrew Burnham MP and requested him to form a new Administration. title: Andy Burnham Becomes Seventh UK Prime Minister In 10 Years
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’ ‘The Rest Is Football’ Returning To Netflix For Next Two Premier League Seasons Holly Willoughby Launches YouTube Lifestyle Series View All July 20, 2026 4:08am Andy Burnham Alishia Abodunde/Getty We’ve been here before. The UK has its seventh Prime Minister in a decade as Andy Burnham arrives at Buckingham Palace to greet King Charles III and be sworn in. The former Greater Manchester Mayor has had an odd coronation, running unopposed to be leader of the Labour Party after his predecessor Keir Starmer stepped down amid dreadful poll ratings, a poor set of local election results and scandal involving former U.S. ambassador Peter Mandelson and Jeffrey Epstein. Starmer delivered his final speech before resigning this morning. Related Stories News Paramount-Warner Deal Faces Weeks Of Uncertainty As British Lawmakers Go On Summer Break News Alan Cumming Urges UK Industry To Help Bring Down Paramount-Warner Merger, As Government Poised To Make Intervention Burnham officially became Labour leader on Friday and Prime Minister today, which is ratified when he meets the King. Once he meets Charles III and is invited to form a government, he will give a speech outside his new 10 Downing Street home. The 56-year-old has been a well-known face in British politics for 20 years. He was Culture Secretary then Health Secretary in the Labour government of the late 2000s before twice unsuccesfully running to be Labour leader last decade. Watch on Deadline His near-decade run as Greater Manchester mayor led his popularity to soar owing to a set of popular policies, which he says he will attempt to roll out nationally, and he is widely seen as being far more charismatic than Starmer. He was a vocal backer of Greater Manchester’s successful bid to host the Edinburgh TV Festival , saying it would “open a new chapter defined by the unmistakable character that only Greater Manchester can bring.” Greater Manchester also houses the city of Salford, home to thousands of BBC employees after what is broadly viewed as one of the most successful out-of-London relocations the nation has ever seen. Burnham’s in-tray is bulging as he moves into 10 Downing Street and he is set to pick his cabinet this afternoon, with the country facing a wealth of enormous challenges. Incredibly for a nation that is only supposed to have two per decade, he is the seventh Prime Minister in 10 years, the majority of whom have stood down rather than lost elections. His recent predecessors include Boris Johnson, Rishi Sunak and Liz Truss. New Culture Sec The film and TV industry will be keeping an extremely close eye on Burnham’s pick for Culture Sec. Lisa Nandy , current incumbent and a close Burnham ally, is reportedly leaving the role to run his new “super ministry” in the north of England. Nandy is in the process of making some seriously big decisions, one being whether to intervene in Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery’s $110B merger, which won’t now be decided until early September . Another is the future funding model of the BBC. Nandy recently signaled her backing for a plan to revamp the BBC license fee so that streaming sevice subscribers have to pay the £180 ($240) annual charge. Other policy priorities include new legislation that would compel YouTube and TikTok to promote public service content from the BBC and other broadcasters. The result of all these decisions could make Nandy’s successor rather unpopular in various circles across the pond. title: Simon Field Dies: Former Rotterdam Film Festival Head & ICA Cinema Director Was 80
full_text: Skip to main content By Zac Ntim Zac Ntim International Reporter @ZacharyNtim More Stories By Zac Giovanni Veronesi’s ‘Dio Ride’ To Close Venice Film Festival ‘S.W.A.T.’ Creator Aaron Rahsaan Thomas & ‘Origin’ Producer Paul Garnes Among Names Set For Inaugural Ghana Creative Economy Initiative Hungary’s Production Incentive Program Reopens After Orbán-Era Pause View All July 20, 2026 4:05am Simon Field Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for BFI). Simon Field , the pioneering film programmer and champion of filmmakers such as Wang Bing, Apichatpong Weerasethakul, and Christopher Nolan, has died. He was 80. Field died on July 16. The news was confirmed by the veteran film critic Geoff Andrew, who posted an obituary of Field on his blog. Andrew was shortly followed by the Rotterdam Film Festival, which shared a tribute to Field over the weekend. Field was Festival Director and Co-Director at Rotterdam from 1996 to 2004. During his tenure, filmmakers who passed through the festival’s Tiger Competition and won awards include Lou Ye, Christopher Nolan, Hong Sang Soo and Pablo Trapero. Related Stories News Jennifer Finch Dies: L7 Bassist Who Battled Brain Cancer Was 59 News John Kirby Dies: Acting Coach, Bruno Kirby's Brother & Bruce Kirby's Son Was 75 Before Rotterdam, Field was a Film Studies writer and lecturer. He was the founding editor of the radical film journal Afterimage , and in 1988 served as the Director of Cinema at London’s Institute of Contemporary Arts (ICA), a position he held until joining Rotterdam in 1996. Watch on Deadline After Rotterdam, Field joined Keith Griffiths’ Illumination Films, where he produced titles by filmmakers such as Tsai Ming-liang, Apitchatpong Weerasthakul, and Garin Nugroho. Field continued to work closely with Weerasethakul over the years and produced his Palme d’Or winner Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives (2010) and his most recent title, Memoria (2012). Weerasethakul shared a tribute to Field in an Instagram post. Weerasethakul wrote: “I have lost an important companion, with whom we built the films together with love, over many years. I cannot dream of a more caring and supportive producer, and a kind gentleman. We were up against changing practices in filmmaking and film funding. But Simon never gave up, nor compromised.” Weerasethakul’s tribute ended: “I will carry him in gratitude, and in the work we still have to finish. Our bond through images will continue to bind others. He is in the light that touches the leaves, young and old. We will continue our journey with the love he showed me.” View this post on Instagram UK Has 7th Prime Minister In 10 Years; Culture Sec To Be Replaced $124M+ U.S. Opening Best Live-Action Haul Of 2026; $264M WW A Nolan Best Spain Beats Argentina For Title; Photos From Closing Ceremony, Halftime Netflix’s Official Most Watched Series Of Recent Years: So, Which One Is No. 1? title: Former ‘This Morning’ Host Holly Willoughby Heads to YouTube for New Lifestyle Series
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 4:05am PT Former ‘This Morning’ Host Holly Willoughby Heads to YouTube for New Lifestyle Series By K.J. Yossman Plus Icon K.J. Yossman @KJYossman Latest Former ‘This Morning’ Host Holly Willoughby Heads to YouTube for New Lifestyle Series 32 minutes ago ‘Boys of Tommen’ Finds Its Patrick Feely in Jamie Schneider, With James Craven Joining as Pierce O’Neill (EXCLUSIVE) 3 days ago ‘The Claudia Winkleman Show’ Canceled at BBC After One Season: ‘I Was Just Too Nervous to Enjoy It’ 4 days ago See All Courtesy of Hungry Bear Media Holly Willoughby , best known until recently as the host of ITV’s daytime show “This Morning,” is set to make a comeback. Rather than return to ITV, however, she has opted to launch her new lifestyle series “Holly Willoughby Together” on YouTube . Premiering July 28, the show is described as a “multi-platform lifestyle series built around real people, real stories, honest conversation, practical advice, celebrity guests, fashion, beauty, health and more” which are apparently “the things Holly genuinely loves.” Related Stories 'Mortal Kombat 2' Sets HBO Max Release Date Does California Attorney General Want to Force Paramount to Sell Off CNN? Rob Bonta Claims 'I Have No Idea' Where That Speculation 'Comes From' The show will be made up of five strands: in-depth coversations, cooking segments, fashion and beauty, health and wellness and, finally, a “hang-out” featuring celebrity guests including Stanley Tucci, Emma Bunton and Katherine Ryan. Popular on Variety Two episodes are set to drop each week on YouTube as well as social media and “digital streaming platforms,” although Willoughby’s rep didn’t reply immediately when Variety asked which ones. The show will be produced by her husband Dan Baldwin’s production outfit Hungry Bear Media, which also makes the hugely successful entertainment show “Gladiators” for the BBC. In a statement, the television host said: “I’m so proud of the work all the team who make ‘Together’ have put into this exciting new project. We’ve had a lot of fun developing it and look forward to seeing what you think.” Willoughby became one of the U.K.’s best-known television personalities thanks to her hosting gig on “This Morning.” But she finally exited the show in 2023 after she found herself caught up in a series of scandals, mostly not of her own making. First there was the “line-jumping” debacle during Queen Elizabeth II’s funeral in 2022, when she and her longtime co-host Phillip Schofield were accused of using their positions to jump the 14-hour line to see the royal’s lying-in-state. That was swiftly followed by the two-part implosion of Schofield’s career the following spring, first when he took a leave of absence while his brother stood trial (and was convicted) for sexually abusing a teenage boy and a short time later when it emerged he had also had an inappropriate relationship with a young staffer at ITV. While Willoughby was not in any way implicated, the collapse of her personal and professional relationship with Schofield inevitably dinged her previously golden reputation. After a brief hiatus she returned to “This Morning” in June 2023 but quit for good four months later after it was revealed she had been the intended victim of a gruesome kidnapping and murder plot. A man was found guilty of plotting to kidnap, rape and murder the star in July 2024. The turmoil led to Willoughby taking a step back from television, aside from a brief stint at Netflix last year on the short-lived “Celebrity Bear Hunt” alongside Bear Grylls, which flopped and was swiftly canceled after one season. Willoughby’s pivot to YouTube comes as the digital platform increasingly dominates viewing figures in both the U.K. and beyond. According to a recent report from U.K. ratings body Barb, YouTube has become a “significant part of the U.K.’s AV ecosystem.” It has also overtaken many of the U.K.’s biggest networks in terms of viewing figures. ‘Cotton Fever,’ ‘Here I’m Alive’ and ‘Summer of Three’ Among 2026 Tribeca Festival Winners Tribeca Festival 2026: The 8 Buzziest Films, From Aubrey Plaza to ‘SNL’ Sakanishi Miiku Makes His Tribeca Debut With a Film About What We Forget, Clip Unveiled (EXCLUSIVE) How I Discovered My Family Connection to the Murder That Inspired ‘Cruising’ Tribeca Kicks Off With Earth, Wind & Fire Documentary and Robert De Niro Dissing Trump as ‘Immoral, Cruel and Corrupt’ Madonna on Technology Ruining the Concert Experience: ‘Put Your F—ing Phones Down and Connect’ Loading comments... title: Venice Critics’ Week Unveils Political, Experimental Lineup
full_text: × Skip to main content By Scott Roxborough Plus Icon Scott Roxborough Europe Bureau Chief sroxborough Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 3:54am 'Rider' Courtesy of Venice Critics Week Venice Critics’ Week, the sidebar section of the Venice International Film Festival , selected by the national union of Italian film critics, unveiled its 2026 selection on Monday. The selection of seven debut features is intended as a showcase for up-and-coming international auteurs. Il Grande Giaffa (Giaffa the Great) from Italian director Marco Santi, the story of an ordinary man drawn into the dangerous illusion of a charismatic performer, will open the section, screening out of competition. Another Italian film, Rider , a star-crossed lovers’ musical set in contemporary Milan, from directors Roan Johnson and Davide Pavanello, will close the section. Related Stories Movies Daniel Ings, Jessica Brown Findlay to Star in Olivia Grant's Directorial Debut, 'The Two Week Wait' (Exclusive) Movies Locarno to Honor James Gray, "the Last Great Novelist of American Cinema," With Career Leopard The competition line-up includes two very different features from Columbia. The experimental documentary essay The End of Times from directors Bibiana Rojas Gómez and Juan David Cárdenas mixes found footage and AI-generated imagery to explore the legacy of historical violence in Latin America. Meteorite from Sebastián Múnera follows two young women on a journey through an extravagant amusement park built on the estate of the late drug lord Pablo Escobar. Other competition entires include the dystopian sci-fi film The H@llow Man from Tunisian director Kamel Laaridhi; London , a drama set around a fetish community, from Brazilian directors Victor Di Marco and Márcio Picoli; and Zoom In, Zoom Out , the feature debut of Chinese director Dong Jie, which follows a young game designer searching through real and virtual worlds to find his missing roommate. This year’s line-up is particularly political, something the organizers said was deliberate, noting that “a festival is not a neutral bubble” separate from the world. But while acknowleging “our cultural activism,” the film critics’ association said artistic and formal criteria were the priority in making the selection. Critics’ Week runs Sept. 2-12 alongside the main Venice festival. Venice will announce its full 2026 lineup on July 23. Check out the full Venice Critics’ Week lineup below. Opening Film Il Grande Giaffa , dir. Marco Santi (Italy) In Competition The End of Times , dirs. Bibiana Rojas Gómez, Juan David Cárdenas (Colombia) The H@llow Man , dir. Kamel Laaridhi (Tunisia) London , dirs. Victor Di Marco, Márcio Picoli (Brazil) Meteorite , dir. Sebastián Múnera (Colombia) Our Share of Sand , dir. Shalini Adnani (India, United Kingdom, Greece) Prima Della Guerra , dir. Tommaso Usberti (France, Italy) Zoom In, Zoom Out , dir. Dong Jie (China) Closing Film Rider , dirs. Roan Johnson, Davide Pavanello (Italy) THR

title: Venice Critics’ Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia
full_text: Skip to main content By Melanie Goodfellow Melanie Goodfellow Senior International Film Correspondent @mel_goodfellow More Stories By Melanie Venice Critics’ Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston Join Guy Pearce, Jared Harris & Merab Ninidze In ‘The Price Of Peace’ As Filming Begins in London James Gray To Receive Locarno’s Honorary Career Pardo Award View All July 20, 2026 3:30am Zoom In, Zoom Out SIC Venice parallel sidebar Settimana Internazionale della Critica (International Critics’ Week) has unveiled the lineup for its 41st edition. The section, organised by the Union of Italian Film Critics (SNCCI) and focused on works by emerging filmmakers, will showcase seven features in competition and two films out of competition. Colombia has a strong presence this year with two works tapping into the country’s turbulent history. Bibiana Rojas Gómez and Juan David Cárdena will compete with The End of Times , a radical essay film using archive footage and artificial intelligence to reflect on memory and colonialism. They are joined by countryman Sebastián Múnera ’s Meteorite , which is described as an intense female-centred story set in a land still haunted by the ghosts of violence. Related Stories News Giovanni Veronesi's 'Dio Ride' To Close Venice Film Festival Festivals Danny Boyle's 'Ink' To Open Venice Film Festival Further competition titles include Tunisian director Kamel Laaridhi’s dystopian drama The H@llow Man , imagining a future in which emotions and memory are controlled by technology, and London , the debut feature of Brazilian directors Victor Di Marco and Márcio Picoli, a queer story that places physicality, desire and the freedom of self-determination at its centre. Watch on Deadline Chilean-Indian London-based director Shalini Adnani is also in the mix with Our Share of Sand , a timely family drama reflecting on inequality and environmental crisis. It was showcased in Cannes this year at the project stage as part of the British Film Institute’s Great 8 initiative . Italy is represented in the competition with Tommaso Usberti’s Prima della Guerra , described as an intense coming-of-age story set in the Po Valley addressing contemporary masculinity with extraordinary maturity. Completing the lineup is Zoom In, Zoom Out by Chinese filmmaker Dong Jie, a work that weaves together mystery, online gaming and urban alienation to capture the anxieties of Generation Z. Screening Out-of-Competition, Marco Santi’s existential noir Il grande Giaffa, starring Edoardo Pesce, will open the section, while Rider , the latest work from award-winning filmmaking and musical duo Roan Johnson and Davide Pavanello, aka Dade, is the closing film. The latter work is described as an urban musical using the language of rap “to explore precarious work, the desire for emancipation and the conflicts shaping a new Italy”. The selection for the 41st edition is curated by General Delegate Beatrice Fiorentino together with committee members Matteo Berardini, Marianna Cappi, Francesco Grieco and Marco Romagna. The 41st Venice International Critics’ Week will take place on the Venice Lido from September 2 to 12 alongside the 83rd Venice International Film Festival. Founded in 1984 by Lino Micciché, has previously showcased the early works of Kevin Reynolds, Mike Leigh, Olivier Assayas, Harmony Korine, Pedro Costa and Pablo Trapero, as well as major names in Italian cinema including Carlo Mazzacurati, Sergio Rubini, Antonio Capuano, Roberta Torre and Gianni Di Gregorio. title: Daniel Ings, Jessica Brown Findlay to Star in Olivia Grant’s Directorial Debut, ‘The Two Week Wait’ (Exclusive)
full_text: × Skip to main content By Lily Ford Plus Icon Lily Ford @lilyfordtweets Follow Sign Up View All July 20, 2026 3:00am Ings and Brown Findlay (center) will star in 'The Two Week Wait.' Pip/Pip Bourdillon/Rankin Fresh off the set of Star Wars: Starfighter , Daniel Ings ( A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms , The Gentlemen ) has joined Jessica Brown Findlay ( Peaky Blinders , Downton Abbey ) in Olivia Grant’s directorial debut, a short film called The Two Week Wait . Fenella Woolgar ( Call the Midwife ), Rick Warden ( Happy Valley ), and Sophie Colquhoun ( Captain America ) co-star in the project, following a woman in her late thirties (Brown Findlay), who comes up against a series of dissociative experiences as she navigates her deteriorating mental health during the rollercoaster of IVF alongside her incompetent, albeit guileless, boyfriend (Ings). Related Stories General News World Cup Final: Spain Defeats Argentina to Take Second-Ever Win Movies '3 Weeks After' Review: A High-School Field Trip Goes Off the Rails in a Skillful but Sadistic Serbian Shocker The Two Week Wait , penned by Grant, is a co-production between her new production banner Cavalry Films and Symphony Films, headed up by actor-producers Rebecca Night ( Wuthering Heights ) and Harry Hadden-Paton ( The Crown ). The film is set to premiere on Tuesday at the Indy Shorts Film Festival. Grant, best known for her acting roles in Stardust , Lark Rise to Candleford and Indian Summers , told The Hollywood Reporter that her desire to start making film came from a mixture of timing and a new sense of urgency. “I’ve wanted to make my own work for a while now and, like many actors, had a feature script I’d been quietly writing for a couple of years, but there was always enough acting work not to focus exclusively on writing, and I was ambivalent about what I would write about. It was COVID that changed that; firstly because it cleared time to focus on generating new work and, secondly, because it coincided with my personal struggles with IVF and its mental toll. As a way to hold on to my sanity, I ended up writing a 30,000-word diary documenting the rollercoaster of grappling with my infertility and transcribing the arguments my husband and I were having, which felt very specific to the process and uniquely gendered.” She continued about her personal experience influencing the process: “It was only as the world opened up again, and I was able to reconnect with more women and couples, that it became clear that many elements of my mental anguish were remarkably universal. My shame around not being able to have a child and what that meant was normal; my partner’s difficulty salving that particular wound for me was perhaps predictable, and the intense sense of dissociation that permeated that period was again part of the experience for many. Like a lot of women, I did not share the experience with my close friends and family and suddenly felt there was a real need to shed some light on what can be the profound interpersonal effects of this remarkably successful, albeit oft-harrowing, process.” The Two Week Wait is a proof of concept for a feature-length narrative Baby Mine , slated for 2027, she added. Ings and Brown Findlay are also attached, with Amy Hubbard on board as casting director. Ings added: “Olivia is a singular new voice in British filmmaking, with a lot to say on some fairly universal themes affecting many women. Getting to work with her, Nanu Segal and Jessica Brown Findlay on this beautiful film was an embarrassment of creative riches. You haven’t heard the last of Olivia Grant.” Night describes the script as “so instantly unique, compelling and beautiful.” She said: “[It] deals with the choices we make, the relationships we foster, and people seeking love and connection as they journey together through life’s struggles. Full of drama, yes, but so witty and heart-wrenching too.” THR

title: Venice Critics’ Week Lineup: Shalini Adnani’s Family Drama ‘Our Share of Sand,’ AI-Assisted Colombian Doc ‘The End of Times’ and More
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 3:00am PT Venice Critics’ Week Lineup: Shalini Adnani’s Family Drama ‘Our Share of Sand,’ AI-Assisted Colombian Doc ‘The End of Times’ and More There are no entries from the U.S., while titles from Italy and Colombia feature prominently By Nick Vivarelli Plus Icon Nick Vivarelli International Correspondent @NickVivarelli Latest Venice Critics’ Week Lineup: Shalini Adnani’s Family Drama ‘Our Share of Sand,’ AI-Assisted Colombian Doc ‘The End of Times’ and More 1 hour ago James Gray to Be Honored With Locarno Film Festival Career Award 2 hours ago Robert De Niro Expected in Rome for 50th Anniversary Screening of Bernardo Bertolucci’s Historical Epic ‘Novecento’ 7 days ago See All Venice Critics' Week London-based Chilean-Indian director Shalini Adnani’s debut feature “Our Share of Sand,” about a 12-year-old girl named Maya who returns to rural India to reconnect with her estranged father, is among standout entries in the upcoming Venice Film Festival Critics’ Week. The section, dedicated to first works from around the world, also comprises two titles from Colombia: the fiction-documentary hybrid “The End of Times,” a political piece co-directed by Bibiana Rojas Gómez and Juan David Cárdenas that uses artificial intelligence and explores colonialism and capitalism in Colombian history, and Sebastián Múnera’s drama “Meteorite,” about plans to build an amusement park on the grounds of the former estate of drug lord Pablo Escobar. Related Stories Netflix Inks Exclusive Deal for Video Episodes of Vox Media Podcasts 'Unexplainable,' 'Switched on Pop' Netflix to Stop Releasing Viewership Data Every 6 Months, Will Shift to Annual Reports Italy also features prominently in the lineup, with Paris-based Tommaso Usberti’s “Before the War,” about a young man named Guido raised within a Northern Italian racist milieu who falls in love with a Kosovar immigrant named Sonia, ensconced in a competition slot. Popular on Variety Also from Italy is the section’s out-of-competition opener, existential noir “Giaffa the Great” directed by Marco Santi, while the closer — also out-of competition — is Italian rap musical “Rider,” co-directed by Roan Johnson and the local music producer known as Dade (Davide Pavanello). “Rider” follows a young food delivery driver who delivers a huge sushi order to a luxurious villa on the outskirts of Milan and is invited to stay for the evening. The seven features competing in Venice Critics’ Week are all by first-time directors and all world premieres. Rounding off the section’s competition are Tunisian dystopian drama “The H@llow Man” by Kamel Laaridhi, set in 2059 Tunisia where a biochip regulates memory and suppresses emotion; Chinese drama “Zoom In, Zoom Out,” by Dong Jie, about a young video game designer who embarks on a journey to seek his missing roommate Gao following a clue hidden in an online game; and Brazilian drama “London,” by Victor Di Marco and Márcio Picoli, about a dilemma faced by a disabled young adult. “Whilst the industry at large is grappling with its crises, these works are proof that cinema never stopped asserting its own cruciality,” said Venice Critics’ Week general delegate Beatrice Fiorentino in a statement. “This cinema is fierce and independent, daring and vibrant, resolute in its reminder that our collective imagination can, and indeed must, continue to be rewritten.” All Venice Critics’ Week competition entries will compete alongside titles in the official selection for the fest’s Lion of the Future prize, which is worth $100,000. The 83rd edition of Venice Film Festival will run Sept. 2-12, with its official selection lineup being announced on Thursday. See all the Venice Critics’ Week titles below. title: Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’ ‘The Rest Is Football’ Returning To Netflix For Next Two Premier League Seasons Holly Willoughby Launches YouTube Lifestyle Series View All July 20, 2026 2:51am UK regulator Ofcom has launched an investigation into under-fire Channel 4 series Married at First Sight UK. Ofcom did not give any more info today beyond saying the investigation has launched and is being categorized under “Fairness and Privacy” after receiving complaints. An Ofcom spokeswoman said: “We are investigating a Fairness and Privacy complaint about Married At First Sight UK .” The regulator’s group director of broadcast previously told British lawmakers Ofcom would examine an “advance copy” of Channel 4’s review into welfare on the show and “urgently” consider “whether “any regulatory action is necessary.” Channel 4’s two welfare reviews into the show have not yet completed. Related Stories News Ofcom Investigating TikTok Over Kids "Being At Risk Of Exposure To Harmful Content" News 'Bake Off' Back On The BBC? Bosses In Talks With "Subscale" Channel 4 To Host Its Content On iPlayer Married at First Sight UK was hit by multiple rape allegations several weeks back and one contestant has been arrested on suspicion of rape . A BBC News investigation found two women alleged they were raped by partners they met on the hit show, while a third claimed she experienced a non-consensual sex act. Ofcom’s Cristina Nicolotti Squires previously told MPs the allegations are “deeply disturbing.” Watch on Deadline In May, Channel 4 opened two investigations , one overseen by a law firm and the other by former BBC One chief Lorraine Heggessey, while removing all MAFS episodes from its streaming service. Lawyers for MAFS UK producer CPL Productions told BBC News they disputed elements of the claims at the time. Channel 4 CEO Priya Dogra apologized in late May to the women who made “very troubling” rape allegations. MAFS UK is one of Channel 4’s biggest hits and has been airing for more than a decade. The show sees contestants meet at the altar for the first time before heading out on honeymoon and subsequently deciding whether they want to remain together. title: Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston Join Guy Pearce, Jared Harris & Merab Ninidze In ‘The Price Of Peace’ As Filming Begins in London
full_text: Skip to main content By Melanie Goodfellow Melanie Goodfellow Senior International Film Correspondent @mel_goodfellow More Stories By Melanie Venice Critics’ Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston Join Guy Pearce, Jared Harris & Merab Ninidze In ‘The Price Of Peace’ As Filming Begins in London James Gray To Receive Locarno’s Honorary Career Pardo Award View All July 20, 2026 2:32am Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston, Guy Pearce, Jared Harris, Merab Ninidze Getty Image Anthony McCarten’s post-World War Two political thriller The Price of Peace – starring Guy Pearce , Jared Harris and Merab Ninidze as Harry S. Truman, Winston Churchill and Joseph Stalin respectively – has begun principal photography in London. The trio have been joined by Mia Threapleton ( The Phoenician Scheme) as Mary Churchill and acclaimed Danny Huston ( Wonder Woman) as General Groves as filming begins. The drama explores the pivotal 1945 Potsdam Conference where the fate of post-war Germany, Europe and the world was decided. Related Stories Acquisitions Netflix Snaps Up Rupert Murdoch Thriller 'Ink' From Danny Boyle Ahead Of Venice Premiere Books Jacob Elordi & Josh Brolin Navigate "Kill Or Be Killed" Post-Apocalyptic World In Final Trailer For 'The Dog Stars' The synopsis reads: “July 1945. In the aftermath of Nazi Germany’s defeat, President Harry S. Truman, Prime Minister Winston Churchill and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin converge at the Potsdam Conference to shape the future of Europe and the world. Each man has a personal and political agenda. Newly appointed Truman needs to end the War with Japan. Churchill is facing a pivotal election back home. Stalin has territorial ambitions over occupied territories. As political tensions rise and personal crises unfold, a high-stakes diplomatic chess game emerges. The three leaders’ decisions and balance of power will impact millions and shape global politics for decades to come.” Watch on Deadline “The Potsdam Conference represented a crucial turning point in world history, the moment when World War II transformed into the Cold War. I’m thrilled to begin production with such an extraordinary cast bringing this pivotal moment to life,” said four-time Academy Award-nominated filmmaker McCarten ( Darkest Hour, Bohemian Rhapsody, Theory of Everything, The Two Popes ). SND, which announced the project at the EFM earlier this year, serves as co-producer and also handles international sales and French distribution. “Following the enthusiastic response from international buyers at the European Film Market in Berlin earlier this year, we’re thrilled to see production underway,” said Ramy Nahas, Director of International Sales and Distribution, SND. “The level of interest from distributors worldwide confirms that The Price of Peace will resonate strongly with global audiences.” McCarten is joined on the production by Director of Photography Henry Braham ( Superman 2025) , Production Designer James Merifield ( Mary Queen of Scots) , Costume Designer Tina Kalivas ( Ballerina) Hair and Makeup Designer Suzanne Stokes-Munton (N osferatu ), Prosthetics Designers Vee Holt and Stuart Richards ( Nosferatu ), Editor Fernando Stutz ( The Two Popes ) and Composer Harry Gregson-Williams ( The Martian ). The Price of Peace is produced by Raccoon Features, Muse of Fire Productions, and Salloway Studios, in association with Spark Productions, Sub Rosa Group and Ingara. Producers are Jonathan Saubach, Philipp Klausing, Matthew Salloway, Anthony McCarten, Eric Falkenstein, Igor Nesterenko, Elizaveta Chalenko and Philipp Nesterenko. Executive Producers include Thierry Desmichelle, Rémi Jimenez, Nicolas Rolland, Simon Moores, Oliver Indictor, Thomas Valentin, Marc Frydman, Ken Davenport, Jonathan Hogue, Gary Hamilton and Ying Ye. Acting as Co-Producers are Ari Benmosche, Michael Durden, Marguerite Hoffman, Carl Moellenberg, Dorit Muzicant, Peter Yu and Cecily Colahan. Associate Producers are Kathleen Fitzgerald, Allan Herzog, Alan Mendelson, Dave Peacock, Courtney Berlin, Willette Klausner, Larry Craig & The Bird, Dave and Cheryl Olson, Julia Willbanks and Jonathan Golub. McCarten is repped by CAA and attorney David Fox of Myman Greenspan Fineman Fox Rosenberg & Light; Pearce, by CAA, Independent Talent Group, Shanahan Management, and David Weber at Sloane, Offer, Weber and Dern; Harris, by Agents Sarah Clossey and Jeremy Barber at UTA and Samira Higham at ITG, Manager Amy Guenther at Gateway Management & Production and Attorney Mark Wetzstein at Sloan, Offer, Weber and Dern. Ninidze is repped by the Curtis Brown Group, through Sarah Spear and Grace Clissold; Mia Therapleton, by CAA in the US and Markham Froggatt & Irwin in the UK, and Danny Huston, by Julian Belfrage Associates, UTA, Untitled and Myman Greenspan Fineman Fox Rosenberg & Light. The U.S. is co-repped by CAA Media Finance. title: Locarno to Honor James Gray, “the Last Great Novelist of American Cinema,” With Career Leopard
full_text: × Skip to main content By Georg Szalai Plus Icon Georg Szalai Global Business Editor View All July 20, 2026 2:00am James Gray Theo Wargo/Getty Images for FLC The Locarno Film Festival will pay tribute to James Gray , bestowing upon the U.S. filmmaker the Pardo alla Carriera , the Career Leopard, presented by Ascona-Locarno Tourism, during its 79 th edition . Gray will receive the award in the Swiss town’s Piazza Grande on Sunday, Aug. 9, and present his latest film, Paper Tiger , after the ceremony. Two key titles by Gray, We Own the Night (2007) and Two Lovers (2008), will also screen in the festival program. “Since winning the Silver Lion at Venice for Little Odessa (1994) at age 25, James Gray has forged a singular career rooted, with piercing realism, in his upbringing in New York’s Russian-Jewish community,” Locarno highlighted. “Films like The Yards (2000), We Own the Night (2007), Two Lovers (2008), and The Immigrant (2013), cemented Gray’s place as one of American cinema’s most artistically distinctive voices – a filmmaker as concerned with criminal underworlds, rendered with exacting detail, as with meticulous family portraiture, often situated within theBrighton Beach of his youth and shaped by a dialogue with classic American and European auteur cinema.” Related Stories Movies Stephen Chow's 'Kung Fu Soccer' Blasts Past $200 Million at China Box Office General News World Cup Final: Spain Defeats Argentina to Take Second-Ever Win Continued Locarno organizers: “As he has established himself at the top tier of filmmakers in the United States, Gray’s explorations of the often elusive promise of the American dream have, over the past decade, expanded into more varied thematic terrain – colonial exploration, space travel, racial prejudice – that reshaped the popular understanding of his work while remaining distinctively his own: The Lost City of Z (2016) with Charlie Hunnam and Robert Pattinson, Ad Astra (2019) with Brad Pitt, and Armageddon Time (2022) with Anthony Hopkins.” Paper Tiger premiered in competition at Cannes this year and will open the 2026 New York Film Festival. “James Gray is the last great novelist of American cinema,” said Giona A. Nazzaro, Locarno’s artistic director. “His films form a rich body of work that echoes the Jewish diaspora, film noir, and Russian as well as North American literature. A filmmaker with a humanist sensibility and profound cinephile knowledge, he has experimented with genres such as science fiction and adventure while retaining his own artistic identity and voice. Celebrating James Gray at the Locarno Film Festival means paying tribute to a unique and unmistakable artist, a filmmaker who speaks the language of cinema and knows how to pass it on to future generations.” The Pardo alla Carriera is being handed out with the support of Ascona-Locarno Tourism, the destination partner of the Locarno Film Festival, which focuses on the development and promotion of tourism in the region. Previous winners of the award include Francesco Rosi, Claude Goretta, Bruno Ganz, Claudia Cardinale, Johnnie To, Harry Belafonte, Peter-Christian Fueter, Sergio Castellitto, Otar Iosseliani, Víctor Erice, Marlen Khutsiev, Bulle Ogier, Mario Adorf, Jane Birkin, Fredi M. Murer, Dante Spinotti, Costa-Gavras, Tsai Ming-liang, Shah Rukh Khan , and, last year, Jackie Chan . The 79 th edition of Locarno takes place Aug. 5-15. THR

title: James Gray to Be Honored With Locarno Film Festival Career Award
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 2:00am PT James Gray to Be Honored With Locarno Film Festival Career Award By Nick Vivarelli Plus Icon Nick Vivarelli International Correspondent @NickVivarelli Latest Venice Critics’ Week Lineup: Shalini Adnani’s Family Drama ‘Our Share of Sand,’ AI-Assisted Colombian Doc ‘The End of Times’ and More 2 hours ago James Gray to Be Honored With Locarno Film Festival Career Award 3 hours ago Robert De Niro Expected in Rome for 50th Anniversary Screening of Bernardo Bertolucci’s Historical Epic ‘Novecento’ 7 days ago See All Getty The Locarno Film Festival will honor James Gray with a lifetime career award at this year’s edition, where the U.S. auteur’s most recent work, “Paper Tiger,” will screen. Gray will receive the Pardo alla Carriera award on Aug. 9 and will present “Paper Tiger” — which launched in May from Cannes — at the fest’s Piazza Grande outdoor venue. Set in 1986, the film follows two brothers, Irwin (Miles Teller) and Gary Pearl (Adam Driver). Irwin becomes entangled with the Russian mob after embarking on a get-rich-quick scheme to clean up the Gowanus Canal, and his former cop brother, Gary, is the only one who can get him out of trouble. “Paper Tiger” will have its North American premiere as the opening night film of the 64th New York Film Festival on Sept. 25. Nazzaro added, “James Gray is the last great novelist of American cinema. His films form a rich body of work that echoes the Jewish diaspora, film noir and Russian as well as North American literature.” He called Gray “a filmmaker with a humanist sensibility and profound cinephile knowledge” who “has experimented with genres such as science fiction and adventure while retaining his own artistic identity and voice.” Previous winners of the Locarno Pardo alla Carriera Award include Francesco Rosi, Bruno Ganz, Claudia Cardinale, Johnnie To, Harry Belafonte, Jane Birkin, Costa-Gavras, Tsai Ming-liang, Shah Rukh Khan and Jackie Chan. title: James Gray To Receive Locarno’s Honorary Career Pardo Award
full_text: Skip to main content By Melanie Goodfellow Melanie Goodfellow Senior International Film Correspondent @mel_goodfellow More Stories By Melanie Venice Critics’ Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston Join Guy Pearce, Jared Harris & Merab Ninidze In ‘The Price Of Peace’ As Filming Begins in London James Gray To Receive Locarno’s Honorary Career Pardo Award View All July 20, 2026 2:00am James Gray Amy Sussman/Getty Images The Locarno Film Festival will honor U.S. director James Gray with career achievement award, the Pardo alla Carriera, at its upcoming 79th edition in August. Gray will receive the award on August 9 ahead of an open-air screening of Paper Tiger on the festival’s landmark 8,000-capacity Piazza Grande. Two other key titles in his filmography – We Own the Night (2007) and Two Lovers (2008) – will also play in the line-up. It marks a first trip to Locarno for Gray who first broke onto the international festival scene in 1994 at the age of 25 with Venice Silver Lion-winning drama Little Odessa , inspired by his own upbringing in New York’s Russian-Jewish community. Related Stories Festivals James Gray's Crime Thriller 'Paper Tiger' With Adam Driver, Scarlett Johansson & Miles Teller To Open New York Film Festival Festivals Twin Sisters Bianca And Dilara Foscht Co-Star In Locarno-Bound 'Manhunt,' Plot Psychological Horror Film 'Oma' He continued to mine New York’s rich migrant culture and criminal underworlds with The Yards (2000), We Own the Night , Two Lovers , and The Immigrant (2013), branching out into other worlds with The Lost City of Z (2016) with Charlie Hunnam and Robert Pattinson; Ad Astr a (2019) with Brad Pitt, and Armageddon Time (2022) with Anthony Hopkins. Watch on Deadline “James Gray is the last great novelist of American cinema. His films form a rich body of work that echoes the Jewish diaspora, film noir, and Russian as well as North American literature,” said Locarno Artistic Director Giona A. Nazzaro. “A filmmaker with a humanist sensibility and profound cinephile knowledge, he has experimented with genres such as science fiction and adventure while retaining his own artistic identity and voice. Celebrating James Gray at the Locarno Film Festival means paying tribute to a unique and unmistakable artist, a filmmaker who speaks the language of cinema and knows how to pass it onto future generations.” Starring Adam Driver, Scarlett Johansson and Miles Teller, Paper Tiger world premiered in competition at Cannes in May and will open the 2026 New York Film Festival in September . The drama, following two brothers whose hopes for a better life draw them into the orbit of the Russian mafia, sees Gray return to the New York milieu of his early work and to the 1980s of his childhood. Gray teamed with his frequent collaborators including Cinematographer Joaquín Baca-Asay, Composer Christopher Spelman, Production Designer Happy Massee, Casting Director Douglas Aibel, Editor Scott Morris, and Costume Designer Amy Roth. Paper Tiger is produced by Gray alongside Rodrigo Teixeira, Anthony Katagas, Raffaella Leone, Andrea Leone, Marco Perego, Gary Farkas, Leonardo Maria del Vecchio, Carlo Salem, Andrea Bucko. Previous recipients of the Pardo Alla Carriere – which is sponsored by local tourism agency Ascona-Locarno Tourism – have included Bruno Ganz, Claudia Cardinale, Johnnie To, Harry Belafonte, Peter-Christian Fueter, Sergio Castellitto, Otar Iosseliani, Jane Birkin, Dante Spinotti, Costa-Gavras, Tsai Ming-liang, Shah Rukh Khan and, in 2025, Jackie Chan. title: Na Hong-Jin’s Cannes Competition Title ‘Hope’ Has Strong Opening At Korean Box Office
full_text: Skip to main content By Liz Shackleton Liz Shackleton pmc-media-editor-2024 More Stories By Liz Na Hong-Jin’s Cannes Competition Title ‘Hope’ Has Strong Opening At Korean Box Office PVR Inox’s Sanjeev Kumar Bijli On India’s Box Office Recovery, Cinema Expansion & Cannes Acquisitions Haul CJ ENM’s Mnet & Universal Music Group’s Republic Partner To Launch Global Girl Group View All July 20, 2026 1:55am Hope Plus M Entertainment Na Hong-Jin ’s Hope , which premiered in competition at this year’s Cannes film festival, raced past 2 million admissions on its five-day opening weekend in Korea, faster than any film released so far this year, according to data from the Korean Film Council (KOFIC). The film, which opened on July 15 ahead of the three-day Constitution Day holiday weekend, passed the 2 million admissions benchmark during the day on Sunday, faster than The King’s Warden which went on to sell 16.9 million tickets to become the highest-grossing Korean film of all time. It also outpaced the opening of Yeon Sang-ho’s Colony , which debuted in Cannes Special Screenings, and passed the 2 million benchmark on the evening of its fifth day. Related Stories News Proposed Merger Of Korean Cinema Chains Lotte & Megabox Collapses News Breaking Baz @ Cannes: How The Filmmaking Esiri Brothers & Sophie Okonedo Put 'Clarissa' In The Spotlight Also supporting Hope ’s opening weekend, the Korean government issued a new round of discount coupons offering $3.90 (KRW6,000) off a ticket just one week before the film’s release. Watch on Deadline Distributed by Plus M Entertainment, Hope stars Hwang Jung-min, Zo In-sung, Jung Ho-yeon, Michael Fassbender and Alicia Vikander in the tale of a small Korean town that encounters an invasion of powerful extraterrestrial beings. The film has sparked significant debate amongst audiences, with 53% of viewers giving it a 9 or 10 rating on Naver, while around a quarter took issue with the lack of world-building, high levels of profanity and ambiguous ending. Na’s 2016 occult thriller The Wailing was also a polarising film but drew close to seven million admissions. Korean press is reporting that the film, billed as the most expensive Korean film ever, would have to sell around 20 million tickets to break even in theatres alone, although it has also secured presales in around 200 overseas territories. Much is riding on the success of the film as Plus M Entertainment is one of a group of companies under JoongAng Group that recently filed for bankruptcy protection . Also opening at the Korean box office over the weekend, Minions & Monsters came in second with 308,576 admissions, followed by live-action Moana with 173,555 admissions on its second weekend, and Toy Story 5 , which opened June 17, with 114,718 admissions. title: ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 1:46am PT ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 2 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 4 hours ago Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share 4 hours ago See All Sagar Pictures Entertainment Sagar Pictures Entertainment (SPE) has confirmed the launch of a global studio business, moving beyond film and television production into an intellectual property-led growth strategy spanning gaming, music, consumer products and theme parks. The Mumbai-based studio said it is transitioning from a traditional content company into what it describes as a future-focused entertainment enterprise, aiming to build franchises and original stories across multiple formats and platforms. Related Stories 'Nemesis' Renewed for Season 2 at Netflix 'Teach You a Lesson' Director Hong Jong-chan and Star Kim Moo-yul on the Netflix Global Hit That Turned a Korean Classroom Into a World Stage: 'Authenticity Is What Travels Borders' “India is home to some of the world’s most timeless stories, many of which remain underexplored in cinema. These narratives are not only entertaining but also carry profound spiritual and cultural wisdom. At Sagar Pictures Entertainment, our vision is to preserve their authenticity while reimagining them through world-class cinematic storytelling for audiences across generations and geographies. This new chapter reflects our commitment to combining a seven-decade legacy with innovation, global collaborations, and future-ready entertainment experiences,” said Akash Sagar Chopra, CEO, Sagar Pictures Entertainment. Popular on Variety “Our first step in this journey is ‘ Shrimad Bhagavatam ,’ a project that reflects the scale and ambition with which we want to approach storytelling. As we build this and future franchises, our focus is on creating stories that can grow beyond cinema into meaningful experiences across multiple platforms, while staying rooted in the authenticity and values that define India’s cultural heritage,” Chopra said. “Shrimad Bhagavatam” is SPE’s inaugural feature film franchise, conceived as a seven-chapter theatrical saga tailored for premium large-format screens. It is a live-action feature adaptation of the “Bhagavata Mahapurana,” one of Hinduism’s eighteen major Puranas centered on devotion to Krishna as an incarnation of Vishnu, alongside cosmology, genealogy and mythology. The franchise is currently in production and follows the studio’s earlier adaptations of Indian epics “ Ramayana ” and the “ Mahabharata .” SPE builds on the legacy of the Sagar banner, founded in 1954 by filmmaker and producer Ramanand Sagar, a recipient of India’s Padma Shri honor. Under the guidance of chair and director Moti Sagar, the group has produced films and television programming known for adaptations of ancient Indian epics, positioning it as a name in spiritual, historical and family entertainment. The company’s productions have reached over a billion viewers in India, per BAARC India and other sources, with reach extending to the global Indian diaspora. SPE operates as the film studio arm of the Sagar Pix Group, based across Mumbai, Bengaluru and Hyderabad. Built on more than 75 years in storytelling, the studio has produced more than 55 television series and over 25 feature films for audiences in India, South Asia, the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region and the global Indian diaspora. Its catalogue includes Indian National Film Award-winning features and award-winning television programming. title: ‘The Rest Is Football’ Returning To Netflix For Next Two Premier League Seasons
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’ ‘The Rest Is Football’ Returning To Netflix For Next Two Premier League Seasons Holly Willoughby Launches YouTube Lifestyle Series View All July 20, 2026 1:20am 'The Rest Is Football' Clifton Prescod/ Netflix Netflix is staying in the soccer game with more The Rest is Football throughout the next Premier League season. The streamer greenlit its first daily sports show and has been airing the Gary Lineker podcast as a vodcast every day during the just-finished World Cup , which ended last night with Spain victorious. This morning, the streamer committed to The Rest is Football for the next two Premier Leagaue seasons starting from next month, airing two eps per week, one on Monday and one Friday. Former Match of the Day host Lineker fronts The Rest is Football with former soccer stars Alan Shearer and Micah Richards, with regular guests including, recently, Gordon Ramsay. The popular soccer analysis pod is in the same stable as The Rest is Politics, The Rest is History and others, all produced by Lineker’s indie Goalhanger . Watch on Deadline Related Stories News Spain Wins 2026 World Cup Final 1-0, Dominating Lionel Messi's Argentina News Tom Cruise Makes Appearance At World Cup Final To "Celebrate A Tournament That Brought The World Together": "This Is Unity & This Is Greatness" The Rest is Football aired during the World Cup from a glitzy studio in Time Square, which Lineker joked before the tournament was just one of the benefits of moving from the BBC, which aired its coverage from Salford in the UK. Lineker exited the BBC under a cloud last year after sharing an Instagram video that contained an antisemitic trope. While not a ratings smash, The Rest is Football performed well, appearing in the top 10 of the UK chart every day of its run and peaking top. Its top-rated episode currently sits 40th on Netflix’s most-watched in the UK of the past seven days, viewed by around 170,000 people, placing it behind the BBC’s Football Daily podcast. Netflix has been leaning in to the sports game recently and just released a tranche of sports docs including on Jose Mourinho, Tyson Fury and Jamie Vardy. Goalhanger boss Tony Pastor said: “Netflix allowed us to preserve everything that makes the show special: sometimes serious, frequently hilarious, but always genuine and warm. We’re delighted that Netflix has enjoyed working with the show this summer and has chosen to extend the partnership for a further two years.” UK Has 7th Prime Minister In 10 Years; Culture Sec To Be Replaced $124M+ U.S. Opening Best Live-Action Haul Of 2026; $264M WW A Nolan Best Spain Beats Argentina For Title; Photos From Closing Ceremony, Halftime Netflix’s Official Most Watched Series Of Recent Years: So, Which One Is No. 1? title: ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 20, 2026 12:05am PT ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 4 hours ago Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share 5 hours ago See All Toonz Media Group New-Gen Universe, Inc. is launching its first consumer products initiative at San Diego Comic-Con, debuting an exclusive collector’s trading card set made with Cryptozoic Entertainment to celebrate its Marvel-distributed comic series. Just 300 convention exclusive boxes exist worldwide for the July 23–26 event, making this among the rarest “New-Gen” collectibles released to date. Designed solely for the convention, the set also builds momentum for the upcoming 3D CGI animated series “New-Gen Nanobot Heroes.” Related Stories LISTEN: How David Fincher and Zach Cregger Secretly Shaped Netflix’s Video Game-TV Hybrid 'Unhinged' Netflix Comedy Series 'I Suck at Girls' Casts Anna Konkle, Taegen Burns and More In the comic, twin brothers Sean and Chris learn their bodies have been enhanced by nanotechnology, thrusting them into a fight against the villain Deadalus within a futuristic world where nature and technology exist side by side. Popular on Variety “New-Gen” was created by J.D. Matonti, Julia Coppola and Chris Matonti and has grown from its Marvel-distributed comic book origins into a transmedia franchise spanning publishing, animation and consumer products. The Comic-Con cards give fans a tangible link between the source comics and the animated series still to come. The initiative is led by Debra Joester, president of Joester Loria Group, who is spearheading New-Gen’s expanding consumer products program. “We are thrilled to collaborate with Debra Joester and Cryptozoic Entertainment on this first New-Gen consumer products initiative. We envision many more imaginative opportunities like these as we work with Joester Loria Group, Toonz Media Group, and world class talent to bring our New-Gen Nanobot Heroes story to children of all ages,” said J.D. Matonti, creator of “New-Gen.” Each box holds a set of 13 collectible cards: 11 base cards drawn from artwork in the comic book series, a single rainbow foil card, and one autograph card bearing the signature of either Matonti, voice actor Molly Fahey or voice actor Tony Oliver. Every autograph card carries an on-card signature and a limited run of 100, and select base and foil cards were produced as unique one-of-one pieces. Buyers at the convention will also receive a free “New-Gen” graphic novel, with a foreword penned by Mark Hamill, that traces the franchise’s origin story. “We are delighted to partner with J.D. Matonti, Toonz Media Group, and the extraordinary talent behind New-Gen Nanobot Heroes,” said Debra Joester. “Fans are craving the next evolution of superheroes and J.D. has created the gripping storytelling, compelling characters and rich, imaginative new world that delivers just that.” Attendees can also preview new artwork for “New-Gen Nanobot Heroes” ahead of its animated debut on the franchise’s website, which will show the heroes touching down by spaceship in the series’ utopian setting. A separate giveaway offers fans the chance to win a graphic novel signed by Hamill. The card release also builds momentum for “New-Gen Nanobot Heroes,” the coming 13-episode, 22-minute 3D CGI series from writer-director Matonti, whose representation includes Evan Anglin and Eric Williams at Zero Gravity Management. New-Gen Universe and Toonz Media Group are producing the show, with Chris Matonti, Julia Coppola, Brad Heisman and J.D. Matonti serving as executive producers. Writers on the project include Brent Friedman, known for “Star Wars: The Clone Wars,” and Eugene Son, who worked on “Avengers Assemble,” alongside four other writers. Toonz Media Group will distribute the series internationally, and Ketchup Entertainment will handle its domestic release. Voice talent set to appear includes Finn Wolfhard, Nick Wolfhard, Anya Chalotra, Luke Wilson, Henry Rollins, Taylor Gray, Molly Fahey, Lena Headey and Tony Oliver, and John Loeffler composed the score. New-Gen Universe is a transmedia company built around the New-Gen IP and other properties; its advisory board counts New-Gen’s own creators alongside former Marvel CFO Ken West and Bonnie Arnold, a producer on “How to Train Your Dragon.” Co-producer Toonz Media Group has spent upward of 25 years in the animation business, turning out an estimated 5,000 minutes annually of CGI and 2D programming, with past credits on “Wolverine and the X-Men” for Marvel, “Speed Racer: The Next Generation” for Lionsgate and “Mostly Ghostly” for Universal. title: Holly Willoughby Launches YouTube Lifestyle Series
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Ofcom Launches Investigation Into ‘Married At First Sight UK’ ‘The Rest Is Football’ Returning To Netflix For Next Two Premier League Seasons Holly Willoughby Launches YouTube Lifestyle Series View All July 20, 2026 12:00am Holly Willoughby Samir Hussein/WireImage EXCLUSIVE: Holly Willoughby is setting up shop on YouTube . The top British presenter is launching Holly Willoughby Together , a lifestyle series for YouTube and social that counts Stanley Tucci , Katherine Ryan, Joanne McNally, Emma Bunton and Nicole Appleton in its first tranche of guests. She will also speak to campaigners like Ellen Roome, who is lobbying for regulation of the tech giants, and chefs including Lauren Leyva. The first ep will launch on July 28 and there will be two per week under five pillars: Talk Together, Cook Together, Look Good Together, Better Together and Hang Out Together. Related Stories News The Ultimate Self-Defense Championship Launching Female-Focused Spinoff Series On YouTube News YouTubers Bolster Netflix Viewing As Ms. Rachel & Mark Rober Score For Streamer But Video Podcasts Struggle To Break Through Filmed in a purpose-built studio, Willoughby will hang out with guests and talk about the week’s chosen topic. Watch on Deadline Produced by Hungry Bear Media, which is run by Willoughby’s husband Dan Baldwin, Holly Willoughby Together is the presenter’s first big YouTube push. According to her reps, she has a digital audience of 16 million. Willoughby is one of the UK’s most popular presenters but she has had a difficult few years, first dealing with the fallout from the scandal around former This Morning co-host Phillip Schofield and then the terror of a man convicted of an alleged plot to abduct and murder her. She has since returned to presenting, fronting ITV’s You Bet! and Netflix’s Celebrity Bear Hunt with Bear Grylls. Willoughby said: “I’m so proud of the work all the team who make Together have put into this exciting new project. We’ve had a lot of fun developing it and look forward to seeing what you think.” The show counts tech health provider Voy as founding brand partner. Numerous top presenters have turned to YouTube in recent years including Piers Morgan, whose hit Talk TV YouTube show now airs a highlights compilation on Paramount network 5. title: Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share
full_text: Jul 19, 2026 11:50pm PT Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 4 hours ago Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share 4 hours ago See All Courtesy of Plus M Entertainment Local science-fiction survival thriller “ Hope ” stormed the top of the South Korean box office during the weekend of Jul. 17–19. According to data from KOBIS, the tracking service operated by the Korean Film Council, the highly anticipated blockbuster generated $11.3 million from 1,504,019 admissions over the three-day window, capturing a dominant 66.62% share of the total market revenue. Related Stories Director Carl Rinsch Sentenced to 30 Months in Netflix Fraud Case After Keanu Reeves and Attorneys Cite Mental Health Struggles Netflix Confirms Ana María Londoño as Head of Content, Colombia (EXCLUSIVE) Directed by Na Hong-Jin , the big-budget production features a star-studded international and local ensemble including Hwang Jung-min, Zo In-sung, Jung Ho-yeon, Michael Fassbender , and Alicia Vikander . The narrative centers on the residents of a remote, isolated harbor village who discover a mysterious alien entity on the outskirts of their town, quickly turning their quiet community into a desperate battleground for human survival. Capitalizing on massive pre-release anticipation and extensive preview screenings, the film has amassed a cumulative gross of $16 million from 2,129,723 total admissions. “Minions & Monsters” debuted in second place, capturing $2.1 million from 308,575 admissions over the frame. Since its launch, the film has gathered a total local cume of $2.6 million from 374,784 ticket buyers. Popular on Variety “Moana” moved to third place during its sophomore outing, collecting $1.2 million from 173,555 admissions. The film has brought its South Korean cumulative haul to $5.6 million from 772,104 total viewers. “Toy Story 5” took fourth place, bringing in an additional $834,989 from 114,720 ticket buyers. The animation has pushed its total local footprint to $19.1 million from 2,750,183 total admissions since its mid-June premiere. Psychological horror-remake “The Eyes” finished in fifth place, generating $552,784 from 75,565 admissions. Directed by Yeom Ji-ho and starring Shin Min-ah and Kim Nam-hee, the thriller has maintained excellent longevity into its fourth frame, lifting its cumulative total to $9.9 million from 1,431,223 total admissions. Faith-based animated musical “David” was in sixth spot, pulling in $436,845 over the frame to push its overall total past the million-dollar threshold. Long-running genre juggernaut “Colony” placed seventh, adding $92,196 from 11,534 admissions. The Yeon Sang-ho viral hive-mind thriller starring Jun Ji-hyun, Koo Kyo-hwan, and Ji Chang-wook has achieved a cume of $42.2 million from 5,941,130 tickets since its May 21 arrival. The Japanese animated feature “Doraemon the Movie: New Nobita and the Castle of the Undersea Devil” followed in eighth place, pulling in $42,042 over the three-day weekend for a cumulative gross of $424,137. Josh Safdie’s table tennis comedy-drama “Marty Supreme,” starring Timothée Chalamet and Gwyneth Paltrow, took the ninth position with a weekend take of $48,551, bringing its cume to $853,810. Rounding out the bottom rung of the top 10 was the Japanese animated franchise entry “Bleach: Thousand-Year Blood War – The Calamity,” which added $30,669 from 3,492 admissions to push its cumulative total to $126,351. title: ‘Shark Tank India’ Sets Season 6 With Parrot Campaign on SonyLIV (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 19, 2026 11:30pm PT ‘Shark Tank India’ Sets Season 6 With Parrot Campaign on SonyLIV (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 2 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 4 hours ago Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share 4 hours ago See All SonyLIV “ Shark Tank India” has unveiled “#DontBeATota,” a campaign taking aim at founders who mistake the language of entrepreneurship for the substance of it, as the show opens its sixth season for entries on streamer SonyLIV . The campaign lands at a moment when terms such as valuation, burn rate and EBITDA have become everyday vocabulary. It poses a pointed question: has the language of entrepreneurship begun to overshadow the business itself? “#DontBeATota” argues that conviction, execution, resilience and a grasp of fundamentals matter more than a polished command of buzzwords. Related Stories 'Nemesis' Renewed for Season 2 at Netflix Netflix Paid $587 Million for Ben Affleck's AI Startup InterPositive The campaign’s mascot is Tota, a parrot who believes he can impress the Sharks simply by reciting startup terminology he barely understands. The character is built around a simple contrast: anyone can memorize the vocabulary of entrepreneurship, but only founders who understand their business can actually build one. Popular on Variety Aman Gupta, a Shark on “Shark Tank India” and co-founder of boAt and founder of Off/Beat, said: “If repeating startup buzzwords was enough to build a business, we would all be investing in parrots. That’s the thought behind this campaign. Over the years, ‘Shark Tank India’ has helped make entrepreneurship part of mainstream conversations, and that’s something we are incredibly proud of. But building a company has never been about sounding like an entrepreneur, it is about solving real problems, understanding your business inside out and showing the resilience to keep building every single day. That’s exactly the kind of founders we’re excited to meet in Season 6.” Founders can now submit entries for the new season through SonyLIV, with the network inviting applicants who can demonstrate real conviction and a clear understanding of their businesses. The Script Room conceived and produced the campaign. title: China Box Office: ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Maintains Dominance as Animated ‘All Wishes Come True!’ Debuts in Second
full_text: Jul 19, 2026 11:16pm PT China Box Office: ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Maintains Dominance as Animated ‘All Wishes Come True!’ Debuts in Second By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest ‘Shrimad Bhagavatam’ Anchors Sagar Pictures Entertainment’s Global Push, Indian Studio Confirms (EXCLUSIVE) 3 hours ago ‘New-Gen’ Universe Launches First Consumer Products Line With Comic-Con Trading Cards (EXCLUSIVE) 4 hours ago Korea Box Office: Na Hong-Jin’s ‘Hope’ Rules With 66% Market Share 5 hours ago See All Shenzhen Film Studio Shenzhen Film Studio’s sports comedy “ Kung Fu Soccer ” comfortably retained the top spot at the mainland China box office during the July 17–19 weekend, generating RMB501.1 million ($73.7 million) in its second weekend of release, according to data from Artisan Gateway. Directed by Stephen Chow , the hit has reached a cumulative total of $214.3 million in its first nine days. The narrative marks a return to Chow’s signature athletic slapstick, following an amateur women’s team as they combine gravity-defying martial arts with professional soccer to storm a major championship tournament. Related Stories TV's Upfront Market Has Nearly Wrapped, Even if Big TV Won't Say So (EXCLUSIVE) Morgan Spector in Talks to Play Robert Langdon in Netflix Series From Carlton Cuse, Dan Brown “Kung Fu Soccer” was the second highest grossing film in the world over the weekend. Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey,” the No. 1 film in the world by some distance, will open in China Aug. 15. Popular on Variety Pearl Studio’s computer-animated fantasy feature “ All Wishes Come True! ” debuted in second place, capturing $30.6 million over the three-day weekend frame. Directed by Mu Zhengyang, the co-production has amassed a total of $39.6 million since launching on July 18. The family-friendly mythological comedy follows eight ordinary mortals who must use their collective wit and courage to outsmart formidable deities, ultimately earning the legendary title of the Eight Immortals. Enlight Pictures’ adaptation of 2015 U.K. film “Miss You Already,” “A Part of Me,” opened in third position, pulling in $13.6 million during its debut frame. Directed by Ma Yingxin and starring Zhang Ruonan and Jolin Jin, the character-driven film spans a multi-decade timeline, chronicling the emotional complexities, personal growth, and shifting bonds of a deeply resilient friendship that crosses 20 years. Universal’s “Minions & Monsters” moved to fourth place in its third week of release, bringing in $3.7 million. The film has now pushed its cumulative Chinese box office haul to $40.3 million since its July 3 debut. Rounding out the top five, a theatrical release of Christopher Smith’s 2009 psychological horror film “Triangle” drew $3.7 million over the weekend frame. Funded by the erstwhile U.K. Film Council, the mind-bending thriller has taken $3.9 million to date. Mainland China’s overall weekend grosses reached $132.9 million, while the 2026 year-to-date revenue stands at $2.97 billion, down 35.3% from the same period in 2025.
