title: Stephen Chow’s ‘Kung Fu Soccer’ Blasts Past $200 Million at China Box Office
full_text: × Skip to main content By Patrick Brzeski Plus Icon Patrick Brzeski Asia Bureau Chief View All July 19, 2026 8:40pm Stephen Chow Keith Tsuji/Getty Images Stephen Chow ‘s perfectly timed return to soccer comedy kept on kicking in its second weekend at China ‘s box office . Kung Fu Soccer , the comedy legend’s Shaolin Soccer spinoff, added $73.7 million (RMB 501.1 million) over the July 17-19 frame, lifting its total to $214.3 million (RMB 1.46 billion) in just nine days, according to data from Artisan Gateway. In a market where second weekends routinely shed half their opening business, the film instead topped its two-day debut frame of $73.6 million (RMB 500.3 million), albeit spread across three days this time. Having crossed $147 million (RMB 1 billion) in its first week, the film has prompted ticketing giant Maoyan to raise its full-run forecast to $441 million (RMB 3 billion), up from the RMB 2.5 billion it projected on opening weekend. The film looks likely to finish just short of Chow’s personal box-office record of RMB 3.39 billion ($500 million-plus), set by the fantasy comedy The Mermaid in 2016. Related Stories Movies 'The Odyssey' Seizes $124M Opening in Box Office Triumph Movies 'The Odyssey' Galloping to $120M in Muscular Box Office Opening Chow, 64, wrote and directed Kung Fu Soccer — his first feature behind the camera since 2019’s The New King of Comedy — but does not appear onscreen, having stepped away from acting after 2008’s CJ7 . Released to mark the 25th anniversary of Shaolin Soccer , and to ride this summer’s FIFA World Cup fever, the film reworks the beloved original’s premise around an underdog, all-female squad whose players integrate martial arts into their game to make an improbable run at a tournament dubbed the Supreme Invincible Cup. Zhang Xiaofei ( Hi, Mom ) leads the cast as the team’s captain, with Dilraba Dilmurat as its star striker and Lay Zhang as its kung fu coach, plus guest turns from Hong Kong screen icon Carina Lau, Japanese star Takeru Satoh and U.S. comedian Jimmy O. Yang. Chow’s singular blockbuster prowess has been demonstrated — yet again — by the way he released the movie with little to no conventional marketing campaign. He set the release date via a casual social media video on his birthday in late June, and the film hit theaters less than three weeks later with minimal advance marketing. A North American theatrical release is expected later this year but remains undated. Singapore’s Encore Films, which handled the international rollout of China’s record-setting animation Ne Zha 2 last year, acquired worldwide rights to Kung Fu Soccer outside the mainland and is lining up territory-by-territory deals. Kung Fu Soccer faced markedly stiffer competition in its sophomore weekend than its first in the form of All Wishes Come True , an animated tentpole from state-backed Pearl Studio, the Beijing outfit formerly known as Oriental DreamWorks, whose credits include Kung Fu Panda 3 , Abominable and the Oscar-nominated Over the Moon . Written and directed by Mu Zhengyang, the comic fantasy reimagines the classic folk tale of the Eight Immortals crossing the sea, following a group of ordinary individuals who outwit the gods to earn their legendary titles. It opened Saturday and brought in $30.6 million (RMB 207.8 million) over two days — a solid start — for an early total of $39.6 million (RMB 269 million) including previews. Pearl and partner CMC Pictures have set a global rollout beginning Aug. 13 in Australia and New Zealand, with the U.S., Canada, the U.K. and other markets to follow from Aug. 14. The rest of the weekend’s top titles trailed the two blockbusters from afar. Enlight Pictures’ A Part of Me , a female-friendship tearjerker starring Zhang Ruonan and Jin Jing — a remake of the 2015 Toni Collette-Drew Barrymore dramedy Miss You Already — opened third with $13.6 million (RMB 92.4 million). Universal and Illumination’s Minions & Monsters held fourth in its third frame with $3.7 million (RMB 25.1 million), nudging its China total to $40.3 million (RMB 274 million). And Triangle , Christopher Smith’s 2009 time-loop thriller starring Melissa George — a curious cult favorite among Chinese movie lovers — took fifth with $3.7 million (RMB 25 million) after arriving in mainland theaters Friday, 17 years after its original release. China’s 2026 box office now stands at $2.97 billion, down 35.3 percent from the same point last year — a comparison skewed by animated mega-blockbuster Ne Zha 2 , which earned over $2 billion in China alone during the first half of 2025. The gap has nonetheless narrowed considerably from the nearly 56 percent deficit of early March, as Chow’s comeback hit has given the country’s summer moviegoing season a healthy kick-start. Curry Barker’s horror phenomenon Obsession , which has grossed more than $400 million worldwide since its mid-May debut, opens in the country on Friday. The film will pose an interesting test of whether the wave of young, internet-bred filmmakers taking Hollywood by storm can help revive the studios’ fortunes in China, where filmgoers have long grown tired of U.S. franchise fare. THR

title: Jennifer Finch Dies: L7 Bassist Who Battled Brain Cancer Was 59
full_text: Skip to main content By Tom Tapp Tom Tapp Deputy Managing Editor More Stories By Tom Trump Booed During World Cup Final Telecast Spain Wins 2026 World Cup Final 1-0, Dominating Lionel Messi’s Argentina Hal Williams Dies: ‘Sanford And Son’ Cop & ‘227’ Co-Star Was 91 View All July 19, 2026 7:48pm Jennifer Finch performs with L7 in 2020 Rich Polk/Getty Images for The Art of Elysium Jennifer Finch , the powerhouse bassist of L7 during the band’s creative peak in the 1990s, has died. That, according to her Instagram account. She was 59. A social media post from the band today noted Finch waged “a long courageous fight with brain cancer.” Earlier this month, friends and family launched a GoFundMe page to help pay for “professional in-home nursing care, physical and speech therapy, medical equipment.” As of today, that fundraiser had raised $395,000 on its goal of $350,000. Related Stories Obituaries 2026 Deaths Photo Gallery: Hollywood & Media Obituaries News John Kirby Dies: Acting Coach, Bruno Kirby’s Brother & Bruce Kirby’s Son Was 75 A Rolling Stone profile of the band once described Finch as “an ill-behaved and untrained bassist who added instant verve to their live act” when she joined in 1986. She left in 1996 in order to get sober and deal with the death of her father. During her first run with the band Finch, who also contributed vocals, wrote the songs “(Right On) Thru”, “Everglade” and “Shirley.” In 1994, Finch and bandmates Suzi Gardner (guitars, vocals), Donita Sparks (vocals, guitars) and Dee Plakas (drums, vocals) played a band called called Camel Lips in John Waters’ Serial Mom , performing the track “Gas Chamber,” which they co-wrote with Waters. Finch also had a small part in the 2017 film Scumbag . She joined Gardner, Sparks, and Plakas in L7 again from 2014 to 2026. Before L7, Finch was in Sugar Babydoll alongside Courtney Love and future Babes in Toyland founder Kat Bjelland. DEADLINE RELATED VIDEO: The band issued a statement today praising “Jennifer Finch, whose fierce spirit, humor and boundless creativity helped shape L7 and changed all of our lives forever. Jennifer was a true original who lived entirely on her own terms, and the impact she made on music, art and everyone lucky enough to know her cannot be measured.” View this post on Instagram The post to Finch’s personal Instagram account read, “We are devastated to announce the death of our partner, sister, daughter, and friend Jennifer Precious Finch. Jennifer’s impact on the world of music was seismic; her impact on our lives, even moreso. We appreciate everyone’s interest and concern, thank you for all the kind messages. We appreciate some space to heal privately during this difficult time.” View this post on Instagram UK Has 7th Prime Minister In 10 Years; Culture Sec To Be Replaced $124M+ U.S. Opening Best Live-Action Haul Of 2026; $264M WW A Nolan Best Spain Beats Argentina For Title; Photos From Closing Ceremony, Halftime Netflix’s Official Most Watched Series Of Recent Years: So, Which One Is No. 1? title: Jim Gilstrap, Singer on ‘Good Times’ Theme and Stevie Wonder’s “You Are the Sunshine of My Life,” Dies at 79
full_text: Skip to main content By Kimberly Nordyke Plus Icon Kimberly Nordyke Managing Editor, Digital View All July 19, 2026 7:28pm Jim Gilstrap Courtesy of Family James “Jim” Gilstrap, a singer whose credits include the Good Times theme song and Stevie Wonder ‘s “You Are the Sunshine of My Life,” has died. He was 79. Gilstrap’s nephew and family spokesperson, Geoffrey “Geoff Gil” Gilstrap, said he died Saturday from natural causes. Gilstrap sang the song for Good Times, which ran from 1974-79, with Blinky Williams. The catchy, gospel-tinged tune (“Keepin’ your head above water/Making a wave when you can”) was composed by Dave Grusin with lyrics by Alan and Marilyn Bergman . Related Stories Music Freddy Cannon, Rock and Roller Behind Hits "Palisades Park" and "Tallahassee Lassie," Dies at 89 Movies John Kirby, Acting Coach and Son of Actor Bruce Kirby, Dies at 75 Gilstrap was a featured vocalist with Stevie Wonder’s Wonderlove and contributed to many of the singer’s recordings. He sang the first two lines of Wonder’s “You Are the Sunshine of My Life” (“You are the sunshine of my life/That’s why I’ll always be around”). Singer Lani Groves sang the next two lines (“You are the apple of my eye/Forever you’ll stay in my heart”) before Wonder’s vocals kick in, with Gilstrap and Groves continuing to sing backup. The song, from Wonder’s 1972 album Talking Book , won the Grammy Award for best male pop vocal performance. Gilstrap also worked on Wonder’s albums Wonderlove and Innervisions . In 1975, he signed a recording contract with Chelsea Records and released a single, “Swing Your Daddy” that reached No. 4 on the U.K. Singles Chart and No. 10 on the U.S. Billboard Black Singles chart. The song peaked at No. 64 in Australia. He released an album, Love Talk, in 1976. Throughout his career, Gilstrap also collaborated with Quincy Jones , Michael Jackson, The Jacksons, Aretha Franklin, Anita Baker, Whitney Houston, James Ingram, George Benson, Bill Withers, Gladys Knight, Luther Vandross, Patti LaBelle, Kenny Loggins, Dolly Parton, Teddy Pendergrass, Stanley Clarke, Boz Scaggs and others. “Jim was one of the most respected session vocalists of his generation, bringing his powerful and soulful voice to countless recordings that helped define the sound of popular music,” his nephew said. Born Nov. 10, 1946, Gilstrap was the last surviving member of a family of 10 siblings. He is survived by his wife, Gloria, and his children. THR

title: ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Fabien Frankel on Criston Cole’s Suicide Mission and His ‘Nihilism’: ‘He’s Losing the Will to Motivate’
full_text: Jul 19, 2026 7:00pm PT ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Fabien Frankel on Criston Cole’s Suicide Mission and His ‘Nihilism’: ‘He’s Losing the Will to Motivate’ By Matt Minton Plus Icon Matt Minton mminton@variety.com Latest ‘House of the Dragon’ Star Fabien Frankel on Criston Cole’s Suicide Mission and His ‘Nihilism’: ‘He’s Losing the Will to Motivate’ 9 hours ago ‘The End of Oak Street’ Preview: Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor Try Not to Get Eaten Alive by Dinosaurs in 14 Minutes of 4DX Footage 20 hours ago The Cure’s Robert Smith Slams FIFA’s World Cup Final Halftime Show: ‘Just F— Off’ 2 days ago See All Courtesy of Theo Whiteman/HBO SPOILER ALERT: This article contains spoilers for Season 3, Episode 5 of “ House of the Dragon ,” now streaming on HBO Max. In tonight’s “House of the Dragon” episode, Ser Criston Cole ( Fabien Frankel ) proclaims that he’s ready to die — and he’s ready to do so honorably. As the war to capture the Iron Throne rages on, Criston marches onward with his men, including Gwayne Hightower (Freddie Fox), to put a stop to the Rivermen reaching King’s Landing and aiding Rhaenyra Targaryen (Emma D’Arcy). But their journey to catch up with the Rivermen proves increasingly difficult, after one of their horses gets caught in a trap. Once they come across a burned-down bridge to cross the river, they realize there is no chance of catching up. Related Stories Alicia Vikander Joins Aaron Taylor-Johnson in 'Enigma Variations' Series at Netflix Netflix Sets Video Licensing Pact With Top Digital Publishers Including Variety, Rolling Stone, Billboard and Other Brands From Penske Media’s PMX Gwayne and Criston regroup and debate what to do next, now down to half the men they started with. Gwayne says continuing forward “would march us toward certain death.” That’s when Gwayne realizes this was Criston’s goal all along, as Criston launches into a revealing monologue: “I have been a servant all my life. To Green or to Black. I have defined myself by oaths, through unyielding devotion. And the highborn have made much of me while it pleased them to do so. But I will not let them diminish me now. I will not return spurned, rejected. I am the realm’s greatest warrior. And I will die as such.” Popular on Variety While Frankel says he doesn’t read fan reactions much these days, it has become easier to tap into his controversial character’s motivations in Season 3: “I can only understand why people feel how they feel about him as a character. It’s tricky for me as an actor, because obviously, I just don’t see him in that way. It serves no purpose for me to see him in that way. You always have to be the bastion of your character, and I feel very much the bastion of Criston Cole.” Frankel spoke with Variety to break down Criston’s suicide mission, why being a soldier is “the only honest version of himself,” and what fans can expect — should the series continue to follow George R.R. Martin’s book “A Song of Ice and Fire,” as expected — from the upcoming Butcher’s Ball. This is a pivotal moment for Criston, proclaiming that he is ready to die an honorable death. Can you talk about his journey throughout this episode, and how he arrives at this point? It’s something that’s been brewing inside of him so much over the course of the show, probably since about halfway through Season 2. These thoughts of, “What am I going to be, and what’s the legacy I’m going to leave behind? In what way do I want to go out?” I suppose that really comes to a head in that fire scene with Gwayne. Does he have a specific vision for how he wants his death to play out? How can he guarantee he’s going to go out the way he wants, and that people will remember him? I don’t know if there’s anything in his head — it’s like he said, “Hell bent in glory,” right? Criston Cole started as a soldier before the 20-year stretch that he spent at King’s Landing — that’s how he started, and that’s how he wants to finish. In his mind, the only honest version of himself is the soldier. Everything else is pretense; the diplomat is pretense; the Hand of the King, there’s a level of pretense; Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, there’s a level of pretense. I suppose they’re all psychological, brain-led jobs. He isn’t that way, he’s very much led by his sword and his fighting instinct. The idea that that’s how he will be remembered, I think, feels honest. Gwayne eventually figures out what’s really going on. Criston is very upfront about his suicide mission once it clicks. There’s a genuine fondness that Criston has for Gwayne; there’s a yin-yang aspect to them. Weirdly, I think Gwayne represents — on the surface — everything that Criston disdains in this world. Yet underneath, Criston’s found someone who will go to battle with him, who does have a heart, who does have empathy, who is much more than he appears. So their relationship is built through this, these guys going to war with each other. You hear any soldier talk about their time in the war, in any war, and they’ll say the relationships they formed are closer than anything you can possibly imagine because you’re living through life and death with these people. You’re risking your lives for each other. As a result of that, there’s a profoundness to their relationship. Theo Whiteman/HBO Why does Criston, then, decide not to tell Gwayne about his suicide mission for this long? I don’t think he’s waiting for Gwayne to figure it out, it’s not a game. Certainly, I didn’t think of it as a game. I think he just knew that as soon as he told Gwayne, that would create a lack of desire to continue. Gwayne’s dislike of this plan would rub off on the rest of the men, and then Criston would lose an army that otherwise would have a good chance of fighting. There’s undeniably a selfishness to Criston’s plan, right? He’s looking out for himself but I think he’s become so accustomed to a world where every single person is looking out for themselves constantly. Unfortunately, any sort of attempt at selflessness falls on itself. For any of the characters, it’s just a very dog-eat-dog world. You’ve spoken in previous interviews about not always knowing Criston’s code, or where his motivations lie episode to episode. As the season has continued on, have you been able to tap into these more? There’s a freedom that comes with nihilism, to a degree. This is a character who, at least at the top of Season 2 and toward the end of Season 3, surrendered himself to the notion that the war is over. That ultimately, we as human beings have no genuinely important part to play in a war that’s ultimately going to be won by dragons or lost by dragons. They are just so much more powerful, and so you meet him at the top of Season 3 in a genuinely kind of — I wouldn’t say nihilistic, because I think that’s a bit reductive. But certainly, there’s an agnosticism to him. He’s losing the will to motivate. He finds that will as the course of the season goes on, and certainly in Episode 4’s planning scene with Gwayne that really gives him the impetus. It re-energizes him and then he certainly has rediscovered his sense of code — not code so much, but a sense of purpose. This episode comes on the heels of Criston finding out that Aemond (Ewan Mitchell) has left him behind, and he’s once again hit with those feelings of abandonment that he’s felt all his life. How does that play into his actions here? It’s tricky, man. I want to think there’s a part of him that is intelligent enough — not intelligent enough — but a part of him that certainly recognizes who Aemond is. I just don’t believe it’s all that surprising that Aemond hasn’t shown, even if he’s playing. Sometimes in order to sell a story as people, we go along with notions that feel like the most natural ones. So Criston saying, “He wouldn’t desert us, I can’t believe that,” I almost feel like that’s him playing into the version of this character that needs that piece of information in order to rediscover a sense of self and purpose. How does he look back on his relationships with Rhaenyra and Alicent Hightower (Olivia Cooke) now — does he have regrets? As he’s spending these long periods traveling to battle, ready to fall on his sword, is he reflecting on his decisions, or is he just focused on charging forward? Haven’t men always done that in history? Just march on, and go to work, “I’m not going to deal with anything that I’m really feeling, I’m just going to go on and get on and do something, and that thing is going to supersede whatever I’m feeling!” But it’s inevitable that Rhaenyra and Alicent are both on his mind all the time. I think Alicent much more than Rhaenyra. Certainly for me, Rhaenyra has become a sort of afterthought. And actually, it’s just the idea of winning the war for Alicent. Has it become easier to tap into Criston’s humanity, despite him being such a controversial character? There’s just genuine insight into who this man is, and what makes him tick. Yeah, I think I have found it easier to tap into the humanity of him. It’s difficult, you can’t undo his actions. He’s done the things that he’s done, and those things have been, at times, very morally reprehensible or questionable, to say the least. And so I can only understand why people feel how they feel about him as a character. It’s tricky for me as an actor because obviously, I just don’t see him in that way. It serves no purpose for me to see him in that way. You always have to be the bastion of your character, and I feel very much the bastion of Criston Cole. How do you look back on your journey on the show and playing Criston? I learned so much from playing Criston Cole. There’s a stillness to him that I don’t naturally have as a person, and I think I’ve learned so much through that. We’re so different as people, but I look back on the last few years with just a ton of warmth towards the people I’ve gotten to work with. Irrespective of playing the character or making the show, it’s the company that you keep that makes any experience. Your former “House of the Dragon” co-star, Milly Alcock, is having a big summer with “Supergirl.” Have you had a chance to see the film, do you keep in touch with Milly at all? I keep in touch with her all the time, yeah! I spoke to her on the phone two weeks ago when she called me from Brazil — I think she was doing press. I’m immensely proud of her, and not in the least bit surprised. I told my agent he should sign her as soon as we did Season 1 of “House of the Dragon” together, and that’s what he did. I’m so happy for her, very proud of her. I haven’t yet had a chance to see “Supergirl,” but I will! I know fans of the book will be looking forward to Butcher’s Ball, which the end of this week’s episode seems to set up. What can you say about the action scenes coming up? All I can say is that it was long and arduous choreography to learn, it’s a brilliantly choreographed fight. There are a ton of unbelievable stuntmen in it that make the fight what it is, and I’m just super excited for people to see that side of Criston. It’s the side of him we haven’t really seen at all — what he is like in a man-to-man situation. This interview has been edited and condensed. title: Donald Trump Booed at World Cup Final
full_text: × Skip to main content By Kimberly Nordyke Plus Icon Kimberly Nordyke Managing Editor, Digital View All July 19, 2026 4:55pm U.S. President Donald Trump (right) and FIFA president Gianni Infantino (second from left) congratulate Lamine Yamal of Spain as they present him with the winners medal on Sunday. Andrew Harnik/Getty Images Donald Trump was booed at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday. The president took the stage at MetLife Stadium (which was referred to as New York New Jersey Stadium for the tournament), located in East Rutherford, New Jersey, with FIFA president Gianni Infantino to present the trophy to tournament champion Spain after the team defeated Argentina in extra time by a score of 1-0. As the two men walked out onto the field, boos could be heard coming from the crowd. On July 6, Trump confirmed that he’d personally intervened to help overturn a suspension for one of U.S. soccer’s star players, striker Folarin Balogun, following a controversial red card against him. Trump said he called Infantino, leading FIFA to suspend the red card decision, thus allowing Balogun to compete in the Round 16 game against Belgium. (The U.S. went on to lose that match by a score of 4-1.) Related Stories General News World Cup Final: Spain Defeats Argentina to Take Second-Ever Win Music BTS Light Up FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show With "Dynamite" Performance The reaction against the move was a mixture of anger and disappointment, particularly among Belgians, as one might expect. The reversal was said to be unprecedented: According to The New York Times, this marked the first time FIFA had reversed a red card since 1962. Trump arrived at the stadium on Sunday in Marine One. The helicopter flew over the stadium about 45 minutes before kickoff. He watched the match from a VIP section behind glass with Infantino. Also sitting nearly were Spain’s king, Felipe VI; Canadian prime minister Mark Carney; and Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum. THR

title: World Cup Final: Spain Defeats Argentina to Take Second-Ever Win
full_text: × Skip to main content By Kimberly Nordyke Plus Icon Kimberly Nordyke Managing Editor, Digital View All July 19, 2026 3:34pm Ferran Torres of Spain celebrates with Lamine Yamal after scoring the team's first goal during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium on July 19. Buda Mendes/Getty Images Spain defeated Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday. Spain won the match by a score of 1-0 after the game went into extra time. Ferran Torres scored the only goal of the match. This marks only the second time Spain has won the soccer football competition. It also took the crown in 2010, defeating the Netherlands by a score of 1-0. The game was also notable as it marked Argentina superstar Lionel Messi’s third time starting in a World Cup final. He’s the only man to have achieved that feat. Related Stories Music BTS Light Up FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show With "Dynamite" Performance TV Jason Sudeikis Brings Ted Lasso to World Cup Halftime Show to Hype Up Justin Bieber The United States, which hosted many of this year’s games, made it to Round 16 before being eliminated by Belgium with a 4-1 loss. Mexico and Canada also served as hosts for the 2026 World Cup, marking the first time in history that three teams have hosted the competition. The 2030 World Cup will not only be hosted by three nations — Morocco, Portugal and Spain — but it also will take place on three continents, another first, as the opening three matches will be played in Uruguay, Argentina and Paraguay. Sunday’s final was played at MetLife Stadium (which was referred to as New York New Jersey Stadium for the tournament), located in East Rutherford, New Jersey. The NFL stadium is home to the New York Giants and the New York Jets. The game was broadcast on Fox and Telemundo. SPAIN LIFTS THE TROPHY AFTER WINNING THE 2026 FIFA WORLD CUP FINAL 🇪🇸🏆 pic.twitter.com/g9LXK96bIQ — FOX Sports (@FOXSports) July 19, 2026 THR

title: BTS Light Up FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show With “Dynamite” Performance
full_text: × Skip to main content By Nicole Fell Plus Icon Nicole Fell Associate Editor nicolemfell Follow Sign Up View All July 19, 2026 3:01pm RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook of BTS perform during the Topps Final Halftime Show in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final match between Spain and Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium on July 19, 2026 in East Rutherford, New Jersey. Kevin C. Cox/Getty Images BTS performed at FIFA ’s first-ever halftime show Sunday, taking part in an 11-minute mega-mix performance during the World Cup 2026 final game between Spain and Argentina. The seven-member global boy group, comprising members RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook, treated the crowd at New York New Jersey Stadium, known outside of World Cup time as MetLife Stadium, with a rendition of their 2020 hit “Dynamite.” BTS joined Madonna, Justin Bieber and Shakira, along with Burna Boy, the New York Philharmonic’s Gustavo Dudamel and The Muppets, in the jam-packed halftime show. Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt also made an appearance as their characters from Ted Lasso . Sunday’s game comes after cities across the United States, Canada and Mexico hosted matches throughout the tournament. Related Stories TV Jason Sudeikis Brings Ted Lasso to World Cup Halftime Show to Hype Up Justin Bieber TV World Cup Final Star-Spotting: Matt Damon, Tom Cruise, Jay-Z, Beyoncé and Timothée Chalamet in Attendance The youngest member of the K-pop sensation, Jung Kook, kicked off BTS’ segment of the show following a nod to Norway’s viking row celebration. The 28-year-old was dressed, much like his other group members in red, black and white, pairing a cool motto jacket with sunglasses. The rest of BTS trickled in as the song continued. BTS opted to modify the lyrics to “Dynamite” to make them fit Sunday’s game with lines like “King Kong, kick the ball” and “Ding-dong, call me on my phone / Ice tea, and a game of football” — the latter sang by V, whose real name is Taehyung Kim, who pretended to kick a ball as he delivered the line. The halftime was produced by Global Citizen with programming curated by Coldplay’s Chris Martin. The non-profit organization has a goal of raising $100 million for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund — each ticket purchased throughout the World Cup has had $1 donated to the fund. The record-breaking boy group is in the midst of a massive comeback year. All seven members returned to group activities last year following their mandatory military enlistment in their home country of South Korea to world on a new album. They released that album, Arirang , in March. BTS is in the midst of a large scale world tour in support of Arirang . They took a break from their string of sold-out shows in Las Vegas in May to take home three American Music Awards in their post-enlistment return to award shows. They’re scheduled to return to Sin City for the iHeartRadio Music Festival in September. The K-pop group will return to MetLife Stadium in just a few short weeks to perform two sold-out shows of their own at the stadium on Aug. 1 and Aug. 2. Watch BTS and the full halftime show below. THR
